text_blocks.text: Luther and his wife moved into a
former monastery, "The Black Cloister," a wedding
present from the new elector John the Steadfast
(1525-32). They embarked on what appeared to
have been a happy and successful marriage, though
money was often short. Between bearing six
December 1527, who died within a few months;
Magdalene - 1529, who died in Luther's arms in 1542;
- 1534; Katharina helped the couple earn a living
by farming the land and taking in boarders. Luther
confided to Michael Stiefel on 11 August 1526: "My
Katie is in all things so obliging and pleasing to me
that I would not exchange my poverty for the
